text.title: Python Async Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Python Async Patterns. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: FastAPI worker stalls under load; CPU low, latency high.
antipattern.root-cause: `requests.get(...)` inside async handler blocks the loop.
antipattern.fix: Replace with httpx async client; add ruff ASYNC rule to lint.

antipattern.symptom: OOMKilled after 30 minutes of high traffic.
antipattern.root-cause: `asyncio.gather(*[fetch(x) for x in large_list])` created 10k concurrent sockets.
antipattern.fix: Wrap in `asyncio.Semaphore(50)` or use TaskGroup with cap; document max_parallel.

antipattern.symptom: Worker pool exhausted after one upstream hang; service goes 503.
antipattern.fix: Wrap in `async with asyncio.timeout(3): await ...` or pass `timeout=3.0` on client.
Re-raise CancelledError explicitly or use `except Exception as e: if isinstance(e, asyncio.CancelledError): raise`.
